records.ety: [L. probabilis, fr. probare to try, approve, prove: cf. F. probable. See Prove, and cf. Provable.]

defs: Having more evidence for than against; supported by evidence which inclines the mind to believe, but leaves some room for doubt; likely.
quotes: That is accounted probable which has better arguments producible for it than can be brought against it.
I do not say that the principles of religion are merely probable; I have before asserted them to be morally certain.

defs: Rendering probable; supporting, or giving ground for, belief, but not demonstrating; as, probable evidence; probable presumption.
